prerequisites: REST API access enabled for the account, with the Client ID and Client Secret available from Settings > API > Client credentials in the SendPulse dashboard. These map to the connector's client_id and client_secret.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials
authMethods.summary: SendPulse authenticates using the OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant. Supply the account's client_id (Client ID) and client_secret (Client Secret). These are exchanged at the token endpoint https://api.sendpulse.com/oauth/access_token with grant_type=client_credentials to obtain a bearer access token, which is valid for 1 hour and sent as an Authorization: Bearer header on each API request.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your API credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to SendPulse and open Settings > API > Client credentials. Copy your Client ID, which is the client_id, and your Client Secret, which is the client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Enter the Client ID as client_id and the Client Secret as client_secret. Both fields are required and are stored as secrets. The connector uses them to request a bearer access token from https://api.sendpulse.com/oauth/access_token with grant_type=client_credentials.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Run the connector. It exchanges the client_id and client_secret for an access token (valid for 1 hour) and authenticates API calls with an Authorization: Bearer token header. A successful token exchange and first request confirms the credentials are correct.
